marmap2 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Read bathymetric data in XYZ format

Source: R/read_bathy.R
read_bathy.Rd

Reads a three-column table containing longitude (x), latitude (y) and depth (z) data.

Usage

read_bathy(xyz, header = FALSE, sep = ",", ...)

Arguments

xyz

three-column table with longitude (x), latitude (y) and depth (z) (no default)

header

whether this table has a row of column names (default = FALSE)

sep

character separating columns, (default=",")

...

further arguments to be passed to read.table()

Value

The output of read_bathy is a matrix of class bathy. Its dimensions depend on the resolution and extent of the input xyz table.

See also

summary_bathy, as_bathy, bathy_to_tbl

Author

Eric Pante

Examples

xyz <- data.frame(
  lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),
  lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),
  depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140)
)

tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
write.table(xyz, tmp, sep = ",", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
bathy <- read_bathy(tmp, header = TRUE)
class(bathy)
#> [1] "bathy"

On this page
